content: Yehezkel (or more properly вҖҳYehezkвҖҷelвҖҷ which means вҖҳGod/El will strengthenвҖҷ, but called вҖҳEzekielвҖҷ in older English translations because Greek has no вҖҳyвҖҷ or вҖҳhвҖҷ) was a priest in Yerushalem (Jerusalem). He was one of the many people who were captured by the Babylonian army and marched off to Babylon before Yerushalem was defeated in 586 B.C. So he lived in Babylon and became a prophet five years after heвҖҷd been exiled. His message was directed to the people exiled to Babylon as well as those back in Yerushalem. YehezkelвҖҷs account can be divided into six parts: 1. GodвҖҷs calling Yehezkel as a prophet; 2. warnings to the people there about GodвҖҷs coming punishment of them, and of the coming destruction of Yerushalem; 3. GodвҖҷs messages about the punishment of various nations whoвҖҷd attacked Israel; 4. comforting Yisrael when enemies defeated Yerushalem, and a promise of a future, better life; 5. the defeat of all of YisraelвҖҷs enemies; 6. YehezkelвҖҷs description of a restored temple and of a nation that would return to living there.

Yehezkel demonstrated a lot of faith, and his messages came as visionsвҖ”many of which he had to act out. Yehezkel explained the importance of a new heart and spirit, and how itвҖҷs necessary for each person to individually repent from their sins. He reveals YisraelвҖҷs hope of a new life. Then as prophet and priest, he greatly looks forward to a new temple with all the rooms that are necessary for the sacred services.
